paper_title: Efficiency of Genetic Transformation via Pollen-Tube Pathway of Jatropha (Jatropha curcas L.) Based on Histochemical and Molecular Analysis

methods.description: PCR analysis was conducted to verify the presence of hptII and gus genes in T0 putative transgenic plants using specific primers and a defined amplification program.

steps.description: Total genomic DNA of T0 putative transgenic plants were isolated by using modified CTAB method.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate genomic DNA using modified CTAB method

steps.description: Specific primers for hptII and gus gene were used for PCR analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PCR with specific primers for hptII and gus genes

steps.description: The amplification program for the hptII gene was as follows: 94°C for 1 minute for initial denaturation, then 40 cycles at 94°C for 1 minute for denaturation, 58°C for 1 minute for primer annealing, and 72°C for 1 min for extension, followed by a final extension at 72°C for 5 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification for hptII gene

steps.description: For the gus gene, the PCR cycle was as follows: 94°C for 1 minute for initial denaturation, then 40 cycles at 94°C for 1 minute for denaturation, 48°C for 1 minute for primer annealing, and 72°C for 1 min for extension, followed by a final extension at 72°C for 5 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification for gus gene

steps.description: The amplified product were separated in a 1% agarose gel and stained with 10 mg L-1 ethidium bromide and visualized under UV gel doc.
steps.searchable_summary: separate and visualize PCR products using agarose gel electrophoresis

methods.method_name: Molecular Analysis of hptII and gus Gene
methods.description: Molecular analysis was conducted to confirm the integration of hptII and gus genes in the genomic DNA of putative transgenic plants, assessing transformation efficiency and challenges.

steps.description: PCR analysis was conducted on the hptII and gus genes that were integrated in the genomic DNA of these plant with hygromycin resistance.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct PCR analysis to confirm gene integration

steps.description: A DNA band was detected at a size of 500 bp and 1,000 bp in the transgenic lines by genomic PCR with primers specific for hptII.
steps.searchable_summary: detect DNA bands for hptII gene
outputs: DNA bands at 500 bp and 1,000 bp

steps.description: Research of jatropha transformation mediated Agrobacterium tumefacient conducted by Li et al. (2008) showed that the transformation efficiency was 13% based on the detection of b-glucuronidase activity, PCR and Southern hybridization analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze transformation efficiency using PCR and Southern hybridization

steps.description: The calculation of transformation efficiency was obtained from the 100 cotyledon explants produced 13 transgenic plants after approximately 4 months.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate transformation efficiency from explant data
